dataset: Nile River corridor survey stations (Aswan to Luxor, Egypt)

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 32 creates this file via heredoc; confirmed via live SSH: file exists with 13 lines (1 header + 12 data), owned by ga:ga, permissions 644
Counted data rows in setup_task.sh heredoc (lines 36-47): exactly 12 stations from Aswan High Dam to Karnak Monitoring Station. Confirmed via live SSH: wc -l returned 13 (1 header + 12 data rows)
Matches task description step (2). export_result.sh greps for 'Nile|Agricultural|Survey' to detect this folder in KML output
Matches task description step (3). export_result.sh greps for 'Nile|Channel|Main' to detect this path in KML output

Task description says 'at least 25 waypoints'. The straight-line distance Aswan-Luxor is 187 km; river course with meanders is ~210-230 km. 25 waypoints = one every ~8-9 km, which is reasonable for tracing a river course at moderate zoom
Aswan High Dam coordinates verified against Wikipedia (24°01'43"N = 24.0286°N). Distance from Wikipedia reference: 10 meters. Also matches first row of CSV data
Aswan High Dam coordinates verified against Wikipedia (32°52'41"E = 32.8781°E). Distance from Wikipedia reference: 10 meters. Also matches first row of CSV data
Luxor Temple coordinates verified against Wikipedia (25°42'00"N ≈ 25.6997°N). Distance from Wikipedia reference: 22 meters
Luxor Temple coordinates verified against Wikipedia (32°38'20"E ≈ 32.6389°E). Distance from Wikipedia reference: 22 meters

0.15 degrees ≈ 16.6 km. This is a design parameter for verification tolerance, not a factual claim. It is generous enough for visual path tracing via map clicking. Consistent with tolerance used in other Google Earth tasks (supply_route_network uses 0.15)
Matches task description step (4). export_result.sh greps for 'Agriculture|Bank|East|West' to detect these polygons

Task description says 'at least 10 vertices'. The cultivation/desert boundary along the Nile is irregular and requires at least 10 vertices to approximate. Confirmed via live satellite imagery that boundaries near Esna and Kom Ombo are clearly visible with sharp green/desert contrast

Computationally verified: all 12 CSV stations fall within bounds. Path start (24.03, 32.88) and end (25.70, 32.64) both within bounds. Bounds provide 9-17 km buffer in each direction beyond the data extent. The Nile river course between Aswan and Luxor stays within these bounds (verified via Google Earth satellite imagery during live testing)

Matches task description step (5). export_result.sh checks this path and common variants (jpg, jpeg). Live testing confirmed /home/ga/Documents/ is writable by ga user and Google Earth's Save Image function (Ctrl+Alt+S) can write to it
Matches task description step (6). export_result.sh checks this path and extracts KML from it via unzip. Live testing confirmed Save Place As dialog can write to /home/ga/Documents/
Standard Google Earth Pro configuration path on Linux. setup_task.sh uses this for baseline state recording. Confirmed via live testing: file exists at this path in the running environment

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a complete GIS survey of the Nile River corridor from Aswan to Luxor, Egypt. The CSV file at /home/ga/Documents/nile_survey_points.csv contains exactly 12 stations with columns: name, latitude, longitude, type, description. The path 'Nile Main Channel' should trace the Nile from Aswan High Dam (24.0286°N, 32.8780°E) to Luxor Temple (25.6995°N, 32.6390°E) — a straight-line distance of 187 km, river distance approximately 210-230 km following meanders. The path must have at least 25 waypoints and be styled blue with width 3. Two polygons should outline irrigated farmland: 'East Bank Agriculture' near Esna (25.29°N, 32.55°E) and 'West Bank Agriculture' near Kom Ombo (24.45°N, 32.93°E). At both locations, satellite imagery shows a sharp boundary between green irrigated farmland and tan desert, which the polygon vertices should trace. Each polygon needs at least 10 vertices and should be green with 50% transparency. A 3D screenshot must be saved to ~/Documents/nile_survey_3d.png with the view tilted to approximately 60 degrees. The complete folder 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey' must be exported as KMZ to ~/Documents/nile_agricultural_survey.kmz. All coordinates are real and verified against Wikipedia geographic references (within 10-124 meters of authoritative values).
